item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article was filled with too many boring cliches to fully enjoy. The awakening was just showing the power of these creatures, the interview was cheesy, and the ending was as well with a bad one-liner.

I know this is supposed to be inspired by the monster movies and Pacific Rim, but it felt like it took the elements of them and added nothing else to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Riemann and Gaffsey have articulated many of my concerns much more eloquently upthread, so I'll keep it succinct. (Narrator: He would not.)I really like the idea of the Foundation dropping the Veil to fight kaijus but this… kind of bungles the execution.

The article opens with the explanation that the Veil has been dropped; so you've wasted a potential "oh shiiiiiit" moment for the end right at the start. The description is fine, but the first addendum is a) boring because reading clinical descriptions of kaiju is boring, but it's an SCP so i can't really fault you much for that, but more importantly b) is literally entirely irrelevant to the rest of the article besides the Crocosquid one and the Wake-02 one. I'm sure they're useful for the rest of the series, but they're just useless wasted space here, and the most important qualifier for any part of a larger series is being able to stand on its own.

The interview log is exceedingly corny and melodramatic — lines like:

(Distressed) And why is that? Is this the future you want? Are you enjoying the destruction of the Veil? The oncoming loss of millions of lives? Your actions have cost us everything. Costed the world everything!

This world is being returned to its rightful masters. I've studied LSAs for decades. These beasts are unstoppable in the purest sense of the word. Fighting them is hopeless. You're better to curl up into a ball and accept your fate. At least for now.

are impossible to take seriously because literally nobody talks like this, it's impossible to visualize that. You're better off cutting the meaning away into subtext and making it an actual back-and-forth rather than Kaori having a mental breakdown and an almost-comically stereotypical Evil Soviet Scientist monologue.

The incident log is a slog because of the aforementioned natural conflict with clinical tone and action, so I'm mostly going to let that one slide. The last addendum would've worked 1000% better as the first time you reveal the Foundation has dropped the Veil - the fact that they've dropped it is entirely irrelevant to the rest of the article so far, so you could've totally kept it in your back pocket until now. I echo Riemann's concerns on the mech line.

I really did want to like this, but its a fantastic premise muddled by an execution of strange choices. I am interested in what comes next though, so you got that one down!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what? An SCP take on literal kaijus vs mechs – that sure as hell gets my upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not so sure I can fully appreciate the article as it currently stands, even within the context of the contest and canon. While the quality of the writing is good, I just can't get behind the Foundation funding a project as outlandish as KEY. Mechs are all well and good, but I don't see how they would be any more effective than existing weapons. The Foundation as I see it has always been built on the values of pragmatism, where projects and containment schemes get sufficient funding, personnel and other resources to operate, but rarely anything excessive. The canon already mentions the use of the Canaba-Howitzer, and more conventional atomic weapons are still on the table, so it doesn't seem reasonable that KEY would be the first to be, if at all, given the go-ahead.

I think a story following the struggling development of a super-weapon as time runs short is far more in line with the common themes of the site, but as is, I'm afraid I don't like it. This may just be my personal gripes, but despite my reservations, I do wish you the very best in the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so excited for this, kaiju are the fucking best man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a joyless pedant, I'm afraid that it's going to be difficult for me to enjoy reading anything which, by one author's admission, was written with "rule of fun over internal logic" as a guiding principle.

And while I might be prematurely judging this canon by its cover, what I'm seeing here doesn't seem to be anything more than Pacific Rim with an SCP coat of paint. (Plus at least one other relevant pop culture reference via Mikasa's namesake, I think.) I can imagine possibly liking a version of this which more deeply explores the interactions between a (less cartoonish) Pacific Rim scenario and the Foundation universe, but this introduction doesn't seem to prefigure the canon going that direction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for pointing that out. I've changed it, as well as the above 1994 error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Appearance: 1998/06/31

There are only 30 days in June.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Description, second paragraph has "1994/06/30". I assume that's a typo? Seems like it should be 1998, and not four years prior to the other events.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohhh I've been waiting for this.

Good shit m8.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would be interested in seeing more tie ins to the rest of the Foundation universe. In this article, Prometheus labs is the source/cause of the first monster, the Foundation and GOC apparently team up, and that’s basically it. Normally, this would be fine, but as a worldwide, Masquerade stretching event with 9 LSAs it feels a little lacking. I suppose I could say that I’m worried about too much focus being put on the LSAs vs mechs, and not enough on the connection to the rest of the canon or universe. The original canon featured a lot of articles from different GOIs to show the wide reaching effects, and some of the other articles in this series help with that, but I would be much more invested in this new series if some future articles had more previously known SCPs and anomalies involved.

Like SCP-4233 “Sea Champion” is basically a mech already, and as a figure of undersea justice and protection this seems right up his alley. Similarly, SCP-2846 “The squid and the sailor” seems to be building up to a similar premise as this new series, so it’d be interesting to see how they react.

The foundation also has access to some anomalous technology, so it’d be nice to see some references to them as inspiration or sources for these new mechs, like SCP-2406 “The Colossus” and SCP-2120 “Damage Control”. I’m worried that the development of the new mechs will get shrugged off with “Oh yeah, there were anomalies involved”, and then they’ll just be generic punching mechs.

Finally, not including SCPs that the Foundation can use or ally with, there are already a ton of SCPs in the ocean. If the non-anomalous is having issue, how are the anomalous faring? SCP-169? SCP-4217? SCP-1264, SCP-2675, SCP-2467? If this really is taking place worldwide, surely some other anomalies would be drawn in and affected, right? I’d really enjoy seeing a tale exploring some these interactions.

Tl;dr: I hope there are more references to existing anomalies and GOIs in future articles. Where are all the oceanic SCPs right now, and what are they doing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was hoping SO hard for a WOAF reboot, this earns my +1 and more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think TheConqueringEnigma is saying that a kaiju fight canon doesn't belong on the wiki. And that's a perfectly valid opinion, in the same way that saying that kaiju fights belong on the wiki is a valid opinion.

More specifically, I think ConqueringEnigma raises a good point in that the sheer outlandishness of this canon does thematically clash with the normal, more secrecy shrouded themes of the 'normal' Foundation. That's not to say it can't be interesting - you pointed to Broken Masquerade - but it does mean there will be some extra work that WOAF needs to do to justify itself to some readers. Personally I think there's no overlap between kaiju fights and the Foundation, so I'm going to be reading these entries looking for ways that the two tie together that's more than a superficial relationship.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who's to say that a kaiju fight canon doesn't belong on the wiki? Black Masquerade exists on the wiki and it doesn't preserve the Veil, if that's something you don't like. Besides, in anywhere that isn't either the middle of the ocean or in Hy-Brasil (which is where the canon was originally set until we split into the Anastasis timeline), it'd be impossible to keep it under wraps.

Anyways, there are no genres that don't belong on the wiki. I don't get your point, unless you just despise kaiju fights that much. Everything belongs on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly just can't get behind this. If it was kaiju sightings being kept under wraps to preserve the Veil with GoC helping it would've been cool, but since it's just a set up for a kaiju fight canon it doesn't feel like it belongs on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Probably one of the worst things I’ve read on the wiki. This is bad and you should feel bad.
Hehe

In all seriousness, this was an enthralling read and a great start to the canon. I'm looking forward to seeing what you have next. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m no-voting on this article, until I’ve had a chance to get better acquainted with the canon that it’s a part of. As the above comment pointed out, there’s a lot stuff that’s straight of a generic kaiju movie, with “This world is being returned to its rightful masters” sticking out as a particularly egregious example. The problem is that all this works in a kaiju movie because it’s all in service of getting everyone to the fireworks factor the spectacle of two CGI/claymation/be-suited stuntmen monsters beat the snot out of one another in glorious, epic detail and scope, right before your very eyeballs.

The problem that I am anticipating from this canon, and which this entry’s description of a kaiju attack has done little allay, is that the epic scope fundamentally does not transfer all that well to prose, particularly the specific format of SCP incident logs. It’s not so much of an issue when it’s a one-and-done story about a giant underwater hell-snake that has some other narrative gimmick to make it interesting, but it appears as though the primary aspects of most of the kaiju here are that they are large and freaky-looking. I'm not particularly excited about reading multiple AAR's where a gundam and a dinosaur beating each other is described in the most clinical and exacting way possible.

I’m very open to being proven wrong, and am withholding my vote for the moment, but I’m a bit skeptical about the project overall and this piece specifically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for articulating your critique. I definitely appreciate that. Yeah, we're going for the Foundation trying something cartoonishly ridiculous because the situation is equally ridiculous. More rule of fun over internal logic. I'd urge you to stick with the canon and try to appreciate it from the perspective of "this is a mecha story set in the Foundation universe" over anything else, because that's what we wanted to write.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still calibrating how I vote on contest entries, because I don't think my normal criteria is going to pass muster. So it's a no-vote now.

I think this is a lot of words to just say Pacific Rim. We've got Kaiju, governments, scientists, blandly evil Russian mad scientists. Normally I think I'd novote or downvote because nothing really happens here and I like self contained articles - but again, that's not a great criteria for contests. I just fail to engage with this because I already knew everything that was going to happen after the first couple lines.

What do people usually do when confronted by giant monsters?

They build mechs.

This line sticks out to me the most - it's definitely supposed to be the hype line, but the inadvertent implications are wild. This means one of two things - either kaiju have attacked the earth before and giant mechs were a valid counter strategy, or more likely… someone saw giant mechs in fiction and are basing their plan to save the world off of a cartoon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice flagship piece. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't know this was already finished! This is a great addition to the canon. Definitely seconding HydrosioN's opinion on the Gundam joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it as a draft and I love it now, excellent work guys

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was a team decision.

And one I am not proud of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was already hella hyped to see this as a draft, can’t wait to see the subsequent entries. +1

Sad to see that the Gundam punchline got removed tho ngl

item.title: Re: choo choo
item.content:encoded: It's definitely inspired by Pacific Rim. That being said, mechs fighting monsters is a story that has existed for decades.

item.title: choo choo
item.content:encoded: The opening notice about the Veil being lifted and the final line about building machines to combat the LSAs serves to hype me up on what will happen in the next episode of War on All Fronts. This might feel like a "ripoff" of Pacific Rim for some people, but I think as an opener to a series and as a standalone SCP, it works as is. I cannot wait to read to read more. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to everyone who looked over this: my team, stephlynch, pedagon, hydrosion, balthazaar, ultimate ridley, and tyumen. Especially Lynch for making that image. I hope this prepares you for the rest of our output.

(images will be localized as soon as possible)
(article 2 coming tomorrow)
